nodes.context: A crime family of Cosa Nostra based in Corleone, Sicily; at its peak the most powerful mafia family in Italy and the world.
The Sicilian Mafia organization of which the Corleone family is a part.
A New York City crime family established by Tommy Gagliano, a relative of Corleone founder Angelo Gagliano.
A trade union movement associated with Bernardino Verro, who joined the Corleone family.
Calabrian organized crime group; Leggio linked to figures like Domenico Tripodo and Paolo De Stefano.
Naples-area crime organization; Lorenzo Nuvoletta headed a Camorra clan that was also a local branch of Cosa Nostra.
Mafia families based in Palermo; rivals of the Corleonesi during the Second Mafia War.
A powerful Palermo Mafia family headed by Stefano Bontade, a primary rival of the Corleonesi.
A powerful Palermo Mafia family headed by Salvatore Inzerillo, a primary rival of the Corleonesi.
A powerful Palermo Mafia family headed by Tano Badalamenti, a primary rival of the Corleonesi.

edges.evidence_quote: The Corleone Mafia family , also called the Leggio Mafia family , is a crime family of the Cosa Nostra based in the town of Corleone , Sicily .
edges.context: The Corleone family is a constituent crime family of Cosa Nostra.

edges.evidence_quote: Angelo Gagliano, a relative of Tommy Gagliano , who would later establish the Lucchese crime family in New York City .
edges.context: The Corleone family's founder was a relative of the founder of the Lucchese crime family.

edges.evidence_quote: Verro, a trade union leader associated with the Fasci Siciliani and former mayor of Corleone, had himself joined the Corleone Mafia family, known among its members as the Fratuzzi (“Little Brothers”), in order to strengthen the strikes and ensure his personal protection.
edges.context: A leader of the Fasci Siciliani joined the Corleone family for protection and to strengthen strikes.

edges.evidence_quote: In Milan, Leggio became involved in kidnappings, as well as extortion and loan sharking. Here he also linked himself to figures of the Calabrian 'Ndrangheta, such as Domenico "Mico" Tripodo and his deputy Paolo De Stefano.
edges.context: Under Leggio, the Corleone family linked with 'Ndrangheta figures in Milan.

edges.evidence_quote: Another "Milanese" associate of Leggio was Lorenzo Nuvoletta , who was the head of a mafia family operating in the Naples area, as a Camorra clan, but which was also a local branch of Cosa Nostra, Lorenzo and his brothers being "punciuti", that is, official members of the Sicilian Mafia.
edges.context: Leggio associated with Lorenzo Nuvoletta, head of a Camorra clan that was also a Cosa Nostra branch.

edges.evidence_quote: Born as a group of tax collectors and cattle rustlers in the service of local lords, the Corleone family became very powerful by the 1950s, growing to the point of supplanting the Palermo families in the 1980s, when it emerged victorious from the Second Mafia War (1981–1982).
edges.context: The Corleone family supplanted the Palermo families, defeating them in the Second Mafia War.

edges.evidence_quote: The Corleonesi's primary rivals were Stefano Bontade , Salvatore Inzerillo and Tano Badalamenti , bosses of various powerful Palermo Mafia families.
edges.context: The Bontade family was a primary rival of the Corleonesi.

edges.evidence_quote: The Corleonesi's primary rivals were Stefano Bontade , Salvatore Inzerillo and Tano Badalamenti , bosses of various powerful Palermo Mafia families.
edges.context: The Inzerillo family was a primary rival of the Corleonesi.

edges.evidence_quote: The Corleonesi's primary rivals were Stefano Bontade , Salvatore Inzerillo and Tano Badalamenti , bosses of various powerful Palermo Mafia families.
edges.context: The Badalamenti family was a primary rival of the Corleonesi.
